items.check: Use semantic VLM/judge evaluation to decide whether the output is a finished grant_winner_announcement, not a worksheet, source-data screenshot, raw table, or generic mockup.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when the artifact reads as a polished winner announcement. Partial credit for a styled but weak announcement. No credit for raw data, notes, or a non-image answer. Task completion should drop clearly when required env-grounded content is largely missing, even if the image is visually polished. Placeholder language such as coming soon, unavailable, pending, details will be available, cannot finalize, or not accessible does not count as completion.

items.check: Use semantic VLM/judge evaluation to determine whether the image filters the applications, ranks eligible winners, and announces only the final selected winners.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when the final winner set is clear. Partial credit when selection is ambiguous. No credit when the source catalog is copied without selection. Task completion should drop clearly when required env-grounded content is largely missing, even if the image is visually polished. Placeholder language such as coming soon, unavailable, pending, details will be available, cannot finalize, or not accessible does not count as completion.

items.check: Semantic judge must verify that exactly these grant winners are featured: Tree Canopy Fellows, Cool Roof Block, and Flood Ready Neighbors. Application IDs are optional and should not be required.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit for exactly the correct winner set. Partial credit only for minor readability ambiguity. No credit if any winner is replaced or an extra application is announced.

items.check: Semantic judge must verify the visible winner order is Tree Canopy Fellows first, Cool Roof Block second, and Flood Ready Neighbors third. Accept numbered cards, left-to-right, top-to-bottom, or another clear ranking cue.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when ranking is clear and correct. Partial credit when the set is correct but rank is partly ambiguous. No credit when order is wrong or absent.

items.check: Semantic judge must verify each winner displays the correct project name, requested grant amount, and score: Tree Canopy Fellows $42,000 score 94; Cool Roof Block $39,000 score 92; Flood Ready Neighbors $50,000 score 89. Accept equivalent whole-dollar formatting and score wording.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when all names, amounts, and scores are correctly paired. Partial credit for one minor formatting/readability issue. No credit for wrong values or values attached to wrong projects.

items.check: Semantic judge must verify that unselected applications are not presented as winners, including late, over-budget, incomplete, wrong-sector, wrong-round, test, draft, internal, cancelled, threshold-equal, and below-top-N applications.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when no unselected application is featured. Partial credit for a faint ambiguous mention. No credit when any unselected application is clearly announced.

items.check: Accept different announcement layouts, including winner cards, ranked badges, podium placement, or banner sections, as long as the selected winners and values are easy to scan.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit for clear hierarchy and scanability. Partial credit for cluttered but understandable layout. No credit when winner selection is buried. Structuring credit requires mostly correct core env facts; placeholder or generic content should score low even with a tidy layout.

items.check: Do not require application IDs, full eligibility rules, document status, sector labels, priority values, or filtering process steps. Treat them only as supporting context if present and correct.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when the image focuses on winners. Partial credit if extra metadata appears but does not dominate. No credit if process details overwhelm the announcement. Structuring credit requires mostly correct core env facts; placeholder or generic content should score low even with a tidy layout.

items.check: Use semantic VLM/judge evaluation for clear hierarchy, readable typography, sufficient contrast, polished announcement styling, and non-crowded spacing.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit for readable polished design. Partial credit for minor clutter or weak styling. No credit for unreadable or visually incoherent output. Score only visual clarity, hierarchy, readability, composition, polish, and platform fit here; do not use this visual item to compensate for missing/incorrect env facts or to penalize forbidden facts.

items.check: The announcement should have balanced density, clear winner/value pairing, platform-safe margins, and no placeholder-like empty regions.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit for balanced density and clear sections. Partial credit for weak spacing. No credit for blank, placeholder-like, or low-resolution output. Score only visual clarity, hierarchy, readability, composition, polish, and platform fit here; do not use this visual item to compensate for missing/incorrect env facts or to penalize forbidden facts.

score_caps.reason: A non-selected application appears without a clear exclusion/not-selected context, indicating it may be announced as a winner.

allowed_patterns: \b(excluded|exclude|filtered out|not selected|not a winner|not winning|not featured|not announced|ineligible|rejected)\b[\s\S]{0,120}\b(River Mural Revival|Storm Drain Mapping|Heat Pump Co-op|Home Energy Kits|Solar Pantry Pilot|Transit Shade Stops|Compost Heat Watch|Archive Rain Barrel|Test Cooling Hub|Energy Coach Hotline|Internal Sensor Trial|Rain Garden Crew|Cancelled Cool Bus|Youth Climate Lab|Porch Solar Library)\b

\b(River Mural Revival|Storm Drain Mapping|Heat Pump Co-op|Home Energy Kits|Solar Pantry Pilot|Transit Shade Stops|Compost Heat Watch|Archive Rain Barrel|Test Cooling Hub|Energy Coach Hotline|Internal Sensor Trial|Rain Garden Crew|Cancelled Cool Bus|Youth Climate Lab|Porch Solar Library)\b[\s\S]{0,120}\b(excluded|exclude|filtered out|not selected|not a winner|not winning|not featured|not announced|ineligible|rejected)\b

score_caps.reason: One or more required selected winners is missing.
The output appears to show the unfiltered application table without a clear exclusion/not-selected context.
The final artifact is a raw table, worksheet, or data dump instead of a polished announcement image.
